evidence.scientific_clarity: All 4 panels match the brief exactly (pipeline diagram, ablation bars, convergence curves, 5x5 heatmap); gridspec(2,3,width_ratios=[2,1,1]) with full-width bottom panel is correctly implemented.
evidence.typography: Script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams['pdf.fonttype']; pdffonts confirms DejaVuSans Type 3 (bitmapped, non-selectable) — the explicit default the rubric flags.
evidence.palette: No red/green used for directional data; Blues sequential colormap for heatmap is CB-safe; Panel C uses gray/orange (good); Panel A uses blue+amber+green which is mostly CB-safe but green (#059669) vs blue (#2563EB) is marginal for deuteranopes.
evidence.layout_density: gridspec hspace=0.42/wspace=0.38 with left=0.06/right=0.97 margins; bottom panel spans full row; ablation bars have value labels; heatmap cells annotated — no gaping whitespace evident from code.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption is strong and matches figure content; panel labels A-D are bold sans-serif upper-left; however Type 3 non-selectable fonts in the PDF would draw an immediate reviewer comment.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows all fonts as Type 3 (Custom encoding, not TrueType/Type1); no pdf.fonttype=42 in script means text is not selectable or searchable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (50 KB), figure.png (135 KB), caption.tex (870 B), gen_figure.py (9.7 KB) all present and non-trivial; figure.svg also produced as bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all four panels with specific findings: '4.2--9.6 percentage points', '64.7%', '30 steps', 'S3--S4 columns'; cites data.json as source; no values mocked.

overall_strengths: Complete 4-panel layout faithfully implements the brief's gridspec spec with a readable conceptual diagram, quantitative ablation bars, convergence curves, and annotated heatmap.
Caption is publication-quality: every panel has a specific numerical claim and the data provenance is stated.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 (or svg.fonttype=0) set anywhere — PDF contains Type 3 DejaVu Sans bitmaps, making text non-selectable and failing the vector-fidelity requirement.

Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout; a named system sans-serif (e.g. Helvetica, Arial, or a bundled alternative like Liberation Sans) should be specified explicitly to avoid the matplotlib default.
